process_name: Flavor-changing neutral-current top decay t -> c gamma
implementation_difficulty_reason: MEDIUM to store the external upper limit, but HIGH for production-grade RS integration because the repo lacks a top electromagnetic FCNC operator basis, RS-to-dipole matching, a t -> c gamma width convention, and a validated production-plus-decay recast.

evidence: Required plan greps rerun on 2026-05-16 across quarkConstraints, qcd, flavorConstraints, neutrinos, yukawa, warpConfig, solvers, scanParams, and tests; no source-code implementation of t -> c gamma, tcgamma, or tqgamma found.

Targeted grep for top-photon FCNC and photon dipole terms found only the lepton-sector mu -> e gamma dipole implementation at flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75 and related prose, not a top observable.
Existing Delta F = 2 coverage starts at quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209.
Modern phenomenology policy enumerates only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 at quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 and states it is policy-only at quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:165.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd warpConfig solvers tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "t.?->.?c.?gamma|t.?->.?gamma.?c|tcgamma|tcgamm|tqgamma|t.?c.?photon|c.?gamma|photon.*FCNC|FCNC.*photon|top.*photon|gamma.*top|dipole" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

status_history.reason: Initial PKA draft and source snapshot deposit.
Writer polish for wa_w7_new_processes; template sections normalized and numerical claims checked against sidecar/source snapshots.

issues: TeX numeric SM prediction B(t -> c gamma) = 4.6e-14 is stored only under paper_era_reference, not under pdg_or_equivalent as required by the CA checklist.

status_history.reason: Addressed CA CHK-1 by removing the exact SM central value from TeX while retaining the order-of-magnitude statement.
CHK-1 failed in cycle-2 checker pass.
issues: T003.tex still states the Standard Model expectation remains at the 10^-14 level; that physics numerical claim is supported only under paper_era_reference, not pdg_or_equivalent as required by the checker checklist.

status_history.reason: Addressed CA CHK-1 cycle-3 finding by removing the explicit 10^-14 SM expectation phrase from TeX while retaining non-numerical wording.
Cycle-3 checker re-check passed; explicit 10^-14 SM-expectation wording is absent from TeX and all CHK-1..8 checks pass.

status_history.notes: Fetched cited CMS/ATLAS/PDG/arXiv sources; tqgamma value claims match.

status_history.reason: Opus round 3 sign-off; full PKA -> WA -> CA cycle-3 (post-L001 precedent) -> fact-check VERIFIED; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_003_index.md.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(t\to c\gamma)\).
This entry catalogs the flavor-changing neutral-current top decay
\(t\to c\gamma\), process id T003 in the top/Higgs/electroweak family.  It
covers LHC searches for a top quark coupled to a charm quark and a photon,
including single-top-plus-photon production and \(t\bar t\) events with one
FCNC top decay.
sections.pdg_prose:
The sidecar headline uses the CMS-TOP-21-013 Run-2 charm-specific result,
based on \(138~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) at \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\)
(\texttt{CMS2024:T003:dataset}):
\[
  \mathcal{B}(t\to c\gamma)<1.51\times10^{-5}
\]
at 95\% CL, with an expected limit of \(1.54\times10^{-5}\)
(\texttt{CMS2024:T003:tcgamma}).  The ATLAS full-Run-2 comparison result used
\(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) at \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\)
(\texttt{ATLAS2023:T003:dataset}) and gave
\[
  \mathcal{B}(t\to c\gamma)<4.2\times10^{-5}
\]
for a left-handed \(tq\gamma\) coupling and \(<4.5\times10^{-5}\) for a
right-handed coupling (\texttt{ATLAS2023:T003:tcgamma\_left},
\texttt{ATLAS2023:T003:tcgamma\_right}).  The PDG live top summary separately
lists a generic \(t\to\gamma q\), \(q=u,c\), row
\(<9.5\times10^{-6}\) at 95\% CL, defined as
\(\Gamma(t\to\gamma q)/\Gamma(t\to Wb)\); that row is not charm separated
(\texttt{PDG2026:T003:gammaq\_summary}).
sections.relevance:
In anarchic warped flavor, the IR-localized top and non-universal fermion
profiles can generate mass-basis flavor violation in the up sector.  The
top-photon channel probes an electromagnetic dipole or heavy-vector matching
direction that is absent from the existing neutral-meson \(\Delta F=2\) lane.
It is therefore complementary to T001 (\(t\to cZ\)) and T005 (\(t\to cg\)):
the same top-charm rotations can appear with different gauge quantum numbers.
sections.post_2008:
Relative to the arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008WarpedFlavor}), the main change is
experimental.  The LHC program moved this channel from indirect expectations to
direct full-Run-2 searches with separate \(tu\gamma\) and \(tc\gamma\)
hypotheses.  ATLAS reached the \(4.2\times10^{-5}\) left-handed charm benchmark
in its 2023 result (\texttt{ATLAS2023:T003:tcgamma\_left}).  CMS then used
\(138~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) and improved the charm-specific observed limit to
\(1.51\times10^{-5}\) (\texttt{CMS2024:T003:dataset};
\texttt{CMS2024:T003:tcgamma}).  The Standard Model expectation is below
current limits by many orders of magnitude, so present bounds are new-physics
searches rather than SM precision tests.
sections.validity:
The upper limit is experimentally clean because the SM decay rate is
negligible.  Its interpretation is model dependent: CMS quotes a single
nonzero anomalous coupling at a time, while ATLAS separates left- and
right-handed \(tq\gamma\) benchmarks.  A warped-model scan would need to map
its top-sector rotations and electromagnetic dipoles into the same operator
normalization before using the number as a hard exclusion.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The required greps across \texttt{quarkConstraints/},
\texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/},
\texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} found no \(t\to c\gamma\),
\(tc\gamma\), \(tq\gamma\), or top-photon FCNC implementation.  Existing
coverage is limited to neutral-meson \(\Delta F=2\) inputs at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209}, the modern policy list
\(\epsilon_K,K,B_d,B_s,D^0\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}, and a lepton-sector
\(\mu\to e\gamma\) dipole check at \texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  Cataloging the external upper limit is straightforward, but a
live RS constraint would require a top electromagnetic FCNC operator
convention, RS-to-SMEFT or direct dipole matching, a \(t\to c\gamma\) width
normalization, and a choice about how to reinterpret production-plus-decay LHC
limits when more than one chirality or flavor coupling is present.
